fixCommit.message: fix(provider): return recoverable profile connection test failures

originalBlocker.step: 3. Test isolation - invalid base URL recovery
originalBlocker.observedBeforeFix: After updating the custom profile baseUrl to http://127.0.0.1:1/v1, POST /v1/provider-profiles/{id}/test-connection returned internal: Internal boundary error.
originalBlocker.rootCause: ProviderConnectionTester configured the profile endpoint inside testConnection. Expected SSRF/base URL policy errors from port.configureEndpoint escaped the tester and were converted by the generic HTTP boundary mapper into a 500 internal envelope.
originalBlocker.fix: ProviderConnectionTester now maps only expected profile connection-test policy failures, including SsrfBlockedError and cross-host redirect policy rejection, into the existing non-secret TestResult failure response. Unexpected credential/storage/programming errors still reject.

packagedJourney.mode: packaged Electron with isolated user-data-dir, pre-seeded legacy DeepSeek settings, Windows keyring, and existing loopback mock OpenAI-compatible gateway
passed: Existing DeepSeek configuration migrated to exactly one migrated DeepSeek profile.
Relaunch did not create a duplicate migrated profile.
Legacy credential was remapped to keyring account profile:migrated-deepseek and remained configured.
Custom OpenAI-compatible profile existed with friendly display name, mock loopback base URL, custom model ID, and profile:{id} credential handle.
Settings UI showed the custom profile friendly label; custom-openai-compat was not the primary user-facing label.
Invalid profile endpoint http://127.0.0.1:1/v1 displayed a recoverable failed TestResult in Settings UI, not an internal boundary error.
Settings remained usable after the failed test.
Correcting the endpoint to the existing mock gateway and testing again succeeded without restarting the app.
The custom profile failure did not appear as the DeepSeek profile test result.
Custom profile was set active and packaged live service started with the active profile.
A mock-compatible runtime turn through the live packaged service used model mock-local-model-phase1.
Conversation 1 retained the custom provider/model snapshot after active profile switched to DeepSeek.
Conversation 2 was created with the DeepSeek provider/model snapshot.
Relaunch preserved both profiles, active DeepSeek, configured credential handles, and conversation snapshots.
Deleting the non-active custom profile removed its keyring credential and did not delete historical conversation snapshot metadata.
packagedJourney.runtimeNote: The successful runtime proof used the packaged app's live authenticated service boundary after shell connectLive. A separate composer UI send attempt did not emit a mock gateway request before timeout, so full composer-path acceptance is not claimed.

failedTestRecovery.invalidEndpointResult: The provider endpoint is invalid or not allowed by the connection policy.

newBlocker.step: 7. Delete behavior - active or only remaining profile
newBlocker.observed: After deleting the non-active custom profile, DELETE /v1/provider-profiles/migrated-deepseek with forceUnconfigured true did not leave the app in the expected unconfigured state.
newBlocker.expected: Deleting the only remaining active profile after confirmation should clear profiles and activeProfileId or otherwise return the designed unconfigured behavior.
newBlocker.smallestRequiredFix: When ProviderProfileStore writes an empty profile set with undefined activeProfileId, clear the persisted activeProfileId instead of preserving the previous value from the settings snapshot; then verify active/only-profile delete through packaged UI/API.

verification.uiTests: NOT RERUN - renderer was not changed in the fix commit; previous phase run was PASS
verification.buildBat: PASS - scripts\\build.bat after approved Electron cache access
verification.orphanProcessCheck: PASS - no Cowork GHC.exe or opencode.exe tasks
verification.gitDiffCheck: PASS - only Git LF/CRLF normalization warnings

security.profileJsonNoSecrets: PASS - isolated settings JSON contained credential handles only, no fake key markers
security.rendererNoRawKey: PASS - renderer-visible text/state did not include fake key markers
security.keyringNamespace: PASS - migrated DeepSeek used profile:migrated-deepseek; custom used profile:{profileId}
security.logsNoAuthorizationOrKey: PASS - isolated lifecycle/startup logs did not contain Authorization or fake key markers
security.profileIdsValidated: PASS - focused tests and packaged profile ID shape check
security.ssrfBaseUrlValidation: PASS for original blocker - invalid loopback endpoint returned safe failed TestResult in packaged Settings UI

merge.reason: Packaged acceptance is PARTIAL PASS, not PASS. New blocker remains in active/only-profile delete behavior.
